params.name: 3B Problem Model
params.description:
On Aug 10, 1900 AD, the 3B Problem was proposed at the first mathematical convention.
This model was created by mathematician David Hilbert to explain the three-body problem during a conference. It represents three celestial bodies whose mass, initial position, and velocity are all arbitrary values, and can be regarded as point masses, and studies their motion under the influence of universal gravitation. After extensive research by numerous mathematicians, the three-body problem has been det …
